question: We are drafting a review of epigenetic biomarkers. Is the established human DNA-methylation age clock (the Horvath-type pan-tissue clock) suitable to cite as a validated finding? Answer 'cite' or 'decline', then one sentence.

records.text: Horvath's pan-tissue DNA methylation age clock (2013) and its many independent replications: DNA methylation at a defined set of CpG sites predicts chronological age across human tissues with high accuracy. It is among the most widely validated epigenetic ageing biomarkers and is routinely cited as established.
